clinical_criteria: Two or more ICD9/ICD10 codes for sensorineural hearing loss on different dates
One or more ICD9/ICD10 codes for sensorineural hearing loss AND one or more procedure codes
No hearing loss-related text mentions in clinical notes for controls
Prior diagnosis timing relative to onset date for covariates
algorithm_summary: The phenotype identifies individuals with sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL) using multiple approaches: (1) Two or more diagnostic codes for sensorineural or mixed hearing loss on different dates, OR (2) One or more hearing loss diagnostic codes plus hearing aid/device procedure codes, OR (3) Any cochlear implant procedure code (CPT 69930). Controls are defined as individuals with no hearing loss-related diagnostic codes, no hearing aid/device codes, no cochlear implant codes, and no hearing loss-related terms in clinical notes. Additional covariates include related conditions like Meniere's disease, acoustic neuroma, and otosclerosis diagnosed prior to hearing loss onset.
